issues.summary: Rare-class narrative overclaimed in Introduction and Conclusion despite underpowered comparison (p=0.884, power=0.20).
issues.rationale: Introduction line 193 states 'yet rare-class performance declines' without qualification. Conclusion lines 693-695 converts underpowered observation into causal narrative about CNN inductive bias. Abstract is appropriately hedged but intro/conclusion are not.
issues.required_action: Harmonize all rare-class language with abstract hedging: 'insufficient evidence' throughout. Condition inductive-bias interpretation on future confirmation.

issues.summary: Novelty claim too broad: Wu et al. (CQG 2025) already reported per-class metrics.
issues.rationale: Manuscript states 'none of these studies has conducted a controlled per-class comparison' but Wu et al. (CQG 42, 165015) report class-wise accuracy tables and confusion matrices. The present paper's novelty is the identical-recipe paired comparison with statistical testing and power analysis.
issues.required_action: Narrow novelty claim to specify identical-recipe paired comparison with statistical testing. Acknowledge Wu et al. per-class results.

issues.summary: Missing canonical review citation: Cuoco et al. (2025) Living Reviews in Relativity.
issues.rationale: This is the current canonical review of ML for GW research. CQG referees will expect it. Additionally Wu et al. cited as arXiv but now published in CQG.
issues.required_action: Add Cuoco et al. (2025) LRR. Update Wu et al. to CQG 42, 165015.

issues.summary: Physical-interpretation claims asymmetrically hedged: Power_Line interpretation presented as explanatory while rare-class interpretation hedged as speculative.
issues.rationale: Neither interpretation has direct evidence (no attention maps, ablations). Asymmetric hedging creates impression of established mechanism where only pattern-matching exists.
issues.required_action: Apply consistent speculative qualification to all inductive-bias interpretations.

issues.summary: CW abstract framing conflates veto efficiency with duty cycle; 'first quantitative assessment' overstates proxy analysis.
issues.rationale: Abstract reports 'approximately equal' veto efficiency without mentioning statistically significant CNN duty-cycle advantage (delta_DC=-0.051, CI does not contain zero). 'First quantitative assessment' overstates a sample-count proxy.
issues.required_action: Abstract must mention duty-cycle difference. Downgrade 'first quantitative assessment' to 'first proxy-level comparison'.

issues.summary: Double-rounding error: ViT O4 macro-F1 reported as 0.670, correct rounding is 0.669.
issues.rationale: Source value 0.6694683 rounds to 0.669, not 0.670. Classic double-rounding via intermediate 4dp value.

issues.summary: '8 rare classes' in power analysis text but only 4 classes meet the rare definition (n_train < 200).
issues.rationale: Power analysis tested 8 classes with small test sets, including non-rare classes. Labeling inconsistency.
issues.required_action: Change 'all 8 rare classes' to 'all 8 classes with small test sets'.

issues.summary: Section heading states absence of correlation as positive finding; Spearman test with n=23 has very low power.
issues.rationale: Absence of evidence is not evidence of absence. Test has power ~0.08 to detect rho=-0.12.
issues.required_action: Soften heading to 'No significant evidence that...'.

issues.summary: 20% O4 degradation threshold is unmotivated and frames asymmetric results as equivalent.
issues.rationale: ViT degrades 7.4% vs CNN 1.7% (4.4x ratio) but both 'pass' an ad-hoc 20% bar.
issues.required_action: Motivate threshold from literature or remove pass/fail framing.

issues.summary: Forbidden-proxy lesson framed as novel without citing imbalanced-learning literature.
issues.rationale: He & Garcia 2009, Buda et al. 2018 established per-class evaluation as best practice over 15 years ago.
issues.required_action: Add imbalanced-learning citations. Reframe as GW demonstration of known principle.

issues.summary: ViT interpretability literature not cited for attention-mechanism claims.
issues.rationale: Discussion argues self-attention captures harmonic structure but does not cite Raghu et al. 2021 or similar.

issues.summary: One-sided p-value for rare-class comparison when observed effect is in opposite direction.
issues.rationale: One-sided ViT > CNN gives p=0.884 when ViT < CNN. Two-sided p=0.232, still non-significant. Conclusion robust.
issues.required_action: Switch to two-sided test or add explanatory footnote.

issues.summary: Wu et al. cited as arXiv preprint but now published in CQG 42, 165015.
issues.rationale: Published version should be cited, especially since CQG is the target journal.
issues.required_action: Update citation to published CQG version.
